notes.note: The consuls named for 43 BC, Aulus Hirtius and Gaius Vibius Pansa; Quintus, unlike his brother, savages them as decadent waverers in the crisis after Caesar's death (the identification of the targets is sometimes disputed).
Mark Antony, who in late 44 BC was marching on Decimus Brutus in Cisalpine Gaul; Quintus fears he will win the consuls over through shared vices.

notes.anchor: the one with Caesena, the other with the foundations of the Cossutian shops
notes.note: Two obscure barbs: Caesena is a town in Cisalpine Gaul, and the 'Cossutian shops' a building venture; the sense is that the two designates could not be trusted with even such trifling charges.
